This drama is terrible. The guy, Gwansik, is a good son, good husband, good father - everything. But there's his wife, who is only obsessed with her daughter and completely ignores her son.

Even Gwansik isn't equal for both their children. If he's a perfect husband, then why isn't he a perfect father for his son? comment_content: I have watched many slice of life dramas, movies, shows, all having some eye-opening messages towards life but this is the first drama which has made me relate to the characters, which actually impacted my life and the way I think about life, even if for a few minutes. I don't know why this drama was so different but it was. I am going to go to college in a few months and I was so excited to start a new life, away from my parents with unlimited freedom. But when Geum Myeong was crying for her family in Japan, it immediately made me relate to her and I realised how much I will miss my family when I go to college. I have never cried so much for a drama as much as a cried when the one who shall not be named died. I felt the regret of that missing last hug. I usually avoid such dramas thinking they would have a boring simple plot. But the boring simple plot of this drama is what made me love it. It wasn't a usual whirlwind romance drama where the male lead and the female lead fall in love, face some struggles, get married and then live happily ever after. This was a drama as mundane as daily life, showing the struggles of a regular couple, of a family, of the truth of life. I cried when they cried, I laughed when they laughed, I worried for them when they were worried. Such dramas are rare to come by and I doubt any drama will be able to replace this one anytime soon.
reply_content: p.s : I blame Gwan Sik for raising my standards so high, I doubt I'll be able to find someone like him.

comment_content:
I’ve never been much of a K-drama enthusiast, and truthfully, I don’t watch them often. But this particular series—When Life Gives You Tangerines—left a profound impact on me. It wasn’t just a story; it felt like a quiet, emotional awakening. As someone born in 2005, the historical backdrop and societal circumstances portrayed in the show were completely unfamiliar to me. And yet, there was something deeply human and universally resonant about it.

Through its characters and narrative, the show gave me a glimpse—however shallow—into the lives my parents and grandparents may have lived. The struggles, the unspoken sacrifices, and the quiet resilience of that era, though far removed from my own experiences, suddenly felt relatable. It made me pause and reflect on the paths they've walked, the hardships they endured, and the love they carried without always knowing how to express it.

What struck me most was how beautifully it captured the chaos of life—how people continued to hold onto hope, relationships, and dignity even when everything around them seemed to fall apart. Watching it didn’t just entertain me—it reminded me that behind every generation is a world of quiet storms they’ve weathered, so that we could live the lives we often take for granted. It’s a rare piece of storytelling that lingers in the heart long after the screen fades to black
reply_content: Beautifully said❤❤

comment_content:
It was my fate that I got to witness this story. I had been watching it for a few days, and today the series ended. I never cry, but today I did—because this story teaches us so much about life, love, friendship, and the love of parents in such a short time. I've been away from home for quite a while, but tomorrow I'm going back to be with my mom and dad. I want to see them, talk to them. I cried a lot today, and now I just want to express all this through writing. No matter what you're going through, make sure to watch this series. I feel so empty today, yet it feels so fulfilling inside. I want to say thank you to every character, every team member. I've never seen anything like this before. I forgot it was just a show; I forgot these were characters…

Thank you.

I've seen a lot in life—sorrow and joy—but I have no regrets. I'm 25 years old now, and someday when I'm 50 or 60, sitting with my wife, I'll remember these moments. I just want to live—with happiness or pain, whatever it brings. I want to live my whole life, see all its ups and downs. Even if I can't give my parents happiness through money, I will surely give them love. My father is everything to me—he has worked so hard. I will stay with him. I have no complaints about my parents—only gratitude. I’m happy.

I love you, Mom and Dad. I may never be able to express it openly, but I am yours—I exist because of you. Please forgive all my mistakes, and stay happy.

Maybe I’ve written a bit too much, but if someone is still reading this—thank you. Keep your parents happy. And love your husband or wife with all your heart. Live life fully. Don't hold on to complaints or grudges…

Thank you.
– Nikhil 🤍

comment_content:
I’ve watched countless dramas in my life, but never has one touched my soul the way this one did. ‘When Life Gives You Tangerines’ didn’t feel like a story—it felt like a mirror reflecting the silent grief that many of us carry but never speak of.

The scene where their child passes… I couldn’t hold back my tears. As someone who has experienced that unbearable loss, this wasn’t just emotional—it was personal. The pain, the emptiness, the unanswered questions—it was all portrayed with such delicate honesty.

Thank you to the creators for honoring such deep emotions with grace and truth. This drama reminded me that grief is not weakness… it’s love with nowhere to go. I pray for every parent who has held their child only to say goodbye too soon. You are not alone. 💔🕊
reply_content: "Grief is not weakness, it's love with nowhere to go" such a beautiful line.

The scene where their child passes… I couldn’t hold back my tears. As someone who has experienced that unbearable loss, this wasn’t just emotional—it was personal. The pain, the emptiness, the unanswered questions—it was all portrayed with such delicate honesty.

Thank you to the creators for honoring such deep emotions with grace and truth. This drama reminded me that grief is not weakness… it’s love with nowhere to go. I pray for every parent who has held their child only to say goodbye too soon. You are not alone. 💔🕊
reply_content: What an incredibly beautiful comment. Thank you for being strong enough to take the time to articulate your reaction to a key part of this series-- and to reach out and offer comfort to others who may have reacted similarly to the show, for similar tragic reasons. For me, it was the detail of the little boy's feet shoved hastily into the sandals, with the heel of the little shoe pushed down to make it into a slipper. The awkward, sweet little body running in anxiety down the muddy footpath... I cried too. "Love with nowhere to go." Thank you.

comment_content:
This drama is a masterpiece, emphasizing what truly matters in life—unconditional love. Unlike other dramas that portray wealth and material success as the key to overcoming hardship, this story reveals a deeper purpose. Watching it, I am inspired to be like Gwan-sik for my family—to love and provide for them so they can rest, even if only for a little while. In death, we finally sleep, but there is beauty in having loved ones worth sacrificing for. That is a life truly worth living.

Spoilers ahead

Together they lived a wonderful life and achieved things people only dream of - had a family, owned a home, a boat, sent their daughter to the best uni in the country, sent her abroad, opened up their own restaurant, provided for their son and grandchildren.
reply_content: I couldn't agree more with you that unconditional love is what really matters in life. The story of three generations of women in this drama emphasizes a mother's unwavering love for her child. In order to prevent Ae-sun from becoming a sea maiden, her mother, Gwang-rye, worked till the end of her life. She wanted Ae-sun to be allowed to follow her ambition of becoming a poet. Despite becoming a housewife, Ae-sun sacrificed everything for her daughter Geum-myeong to be able to attend college and study overseas. In addition to that, Gwan-sik has given his all to the family as a husband and a father. As a husband, he supports his wife unconditionally and gives her love and care. As a father, he supported his family by doing dangerous fishing work and sold his house to give his daughter a better education. Although he worked hard throughout his life and only lived to be 56, his death was tragic for many people since they believed he should have had a comfortable and happy old age. We all sleep long, but having loved ones worth dying for is something that makes life worth living.

comment_content:
I must say, this drama has left an indelible mark on my heart. It's undoubtedly one of the best dramas I've watched in recent times. The storyline is expertly woven, showcasing the most beautiful aspects of human relationships.

At its core, this drama is a poignant portrayal of true love. The way the characters' emotions are explored, the depth of their feelings, and the sacrifices they make for each other are all truly captivating.

What's more, the drama highlights the importance of mutual respect in relationships. The characters' interactions are laced with empathy, understanding, and a deep appreciation for one another.

The parental love depicted in the drama is also noteworthy. The sacrifices that the parents make for their children, the unconditional support they provide, and the wisdom they impart are all heartwarming.

Overall, this drama is a masterpiece that will leave you feeling hopeful, inspired, and perhaps even a little introspective. If you haven't watched it yet, I highly recommend adding it to your must-watch list!"

Finally watched all the episodes of When Life Gives You Tangerines yesterday, and I was completely swept away from the start. Every moment hit me right in the heart. I laughed through the warmth, cried through the pain, and felt so deeply connected to every character’s journey. I do hope for a second season, but at the same time, there’s something perfect about the way it ended. It was raw, tender, and hauntingly beautiful… easily one of the most unforgettable Korean dramas I’ve ever experienced.

As a Korean American, the story resonated with me on an even deeper level. My parents went through a similar path, and watching this felt like revisiting pieces of their past. It already feels like a nostalgic memory I’ll carry with me for a long time. A++
